description: Phase 12.8 — Batch 2 Direct-Email Outreach Candidate Research
method: Direct email discovery via curl/Python requests — contact pages and about pages scraped for publicly visible emails

candidates.name: Real Estate Witch
candidates.about: Consumer-focused real estate blog covering home buying, selling, and mortgage topics. Owned by Clever Real Estate.
candidates.why_fit: Audience is directly homebuyers and sellers — perfect for a mortgage affordability calculator. Blog covers mortgage rates, affordability, and first-time homebuyer guides.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your readers are literally in the market for homes — give them a free, embeddable affordability calculator right on your articles

candidates.name: Springboard (Credit.org)
candidates.about: Non-profit credit counseling organization offering housing counseling, mortgage assistance, and financial education. ACCREDITED by COA.
candidates.why_fit: Directly provides housing counseling and mortgage education. A free mortgage affordability calculator would be a natural addition to their resource toolkit for counselors and clients.
candidates.outreach_angle: Free, no-signup mortgage affordability calculator for your housing counselors and their clients — uses standard DTI ratios, already built and embeddable

candidates.name: Physician on FIRE
candidates.about: Popular FIRE blog for physicians and high-income professionals. Covers investing, real estate, mortgages, and financial independence.
candidates.why_fit: Large FIRE audience interested in real estate investing and mortgage optimization. Has mortgage-specific content on the blog. High-income audience buying expensive homes.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your readers are making big housing decisions — embed our free affordability calculator so they can instantly see what they qualify for based on income and debt

candidates.name: Greenpath Financial Wellness
candidates.about: National non-profit providing financial wellness services including housing counseling, mortgage assistance, and homebuyer education. NFCC member.
candidates.why_fit: Offers HUD-approved housing counseling, mortgage delinquency assistance, and first-time homebuyer programs. Calculator would serve their counselors and clients directly.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your housing counselors already help clients with affordability math — our free embeddable calculator automates this, saving counselor time and letting clients explore scenarios independently
candidates.email_caveat: mediarelations@ is media/PR contact — may forward to correct department or may be wrong channel

candidates.name: Young and the Invested
candidates.about: Personal finance and investing blog for young professionals. Covers first-time homebuying, real estate investing, and financial planning for millennials.
candidates.why_fit: Audience is young professionals considering first home purchases. Blog owner is directly reachable — higher response probability from personal blogs. Covers real estate and homebuying.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your audience is at prime first-home-buying age — a free affordability calculator on your site gives them instant clarity on what they can afford

candidates.name: Take Charge America
candidates.about: Non-profit credit counseling and financial education organization. Offers housing counseling, mortgage assistance, and homebuyer education programs.
candidates.why_fit: Provides housing counseling and mortgage education. Free calculator would complement their existing financial tools and resources for clients.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your financial counselors guide clients through housing decisions — our widget gives them a free, always-available affordability calculator to share

candidates.name: Fiscal Tiger
candidates.about: Personal finance blog with calculators and tools. Covers budgeting, credit, loans, and mortgages.
candidates.why_fit: Already features financial tools and calculators — a mortgage affordability widget would fit naturally into their existing tools section. Mortgage content present.
candidates.outreach_angle: You already offer financial calculators — add our free mortgage affordability widget to complete your tools collection for readers

candidates.name: The Ways To Wealth
candidates.about: Personal finance blog by RJ Weiss, CFP. Covers investing, budgeting, mortgages, and financial planning. Well-established with years of content.
candidates.why_fit: CFP-run blog with mortgage and real estate content. Direct contact with the owner (RJ Weiss) — high chance of personal response. Audience interested in financial planning including housing.
candidates.outreach_angle: As a CFP, you know housing is most people's biggest expense — our free widget helps your readers instantly calculate mortgage affordability with real DTI ratios

candidates.name: Family Credit Management
candidates.about: Non-profit credit counseling agency providing debt management, housing counseling, and financial education to families.
candidates.why_fit: Offers housing counseling and mortgage assistance programs. Calculator would support their counseling services.
candidates.outreach_angle: Free affordability calculator for your credit and housing counselors to use with clients exploring homeownership

candidates.name: Roost Real Estate Co
candidates.about: Local real estate brokerage blog covering home buying and selling. Local market focus.
candidates.why_fit: Real estate brokerage blog — audience is homebuyers and sellers. Local market focus makes affordability calculator highly relevant for their readers.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your clients are buying homes — give them a free affordability calculator on your blog so they know what they qualify for before contacting an agent

candidates.name: Mortgage News Daily
candidates.about: Leading mortgage rate news and analysis site. Daily rate updates, market commentary, and mortgage calculators already on site.
candidates.why_fit: Directly mortgage-focused with existing calculator tools. Highly relevant audience. email is support@ which may not reach editorial/content team.
candidates.outreach_angle: You already feature mortgage tools — our embeddable affordability calculator would complement your existing rate tools
candidates.email_caveat: support@ likely goes to tech support, not content team

candidates.name: Inman (Real Estate News)
candidates.about: Leading real estate news publication for industry professionals. Massive audience of agents, brokers, and mortgage professionals.
candidates.why_fit: Huge authority, real estate focus. But audience is industry professionals not consumers — lower widget embed likelihood from a news publication.
candidates.outreach_angle: Mortgage professionals in your audience could use this free widget on their own sites — free embed for the industry

candidates.name: Keeping Current Matters
candidates.about: Real estate market insights and content for agents. Provides market updates that agents share with their clients.
candidates.why_fit: Real estate agent-focused. Agents might embed the calculator on their own sites — but indirect audience. KCM provides content for agents, not consumers.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your agent subscribers need tools for their own sites — recommend our free affordability widget as a value-add for their clients

candidates.name: Home Bay (Clever)
candidates.about: Home buying and selling resource site by Clever Real Estate. Covers homebuying process, mortgage guides, and market data.
candidates.why_fit: Consumer homebuying resource with mortgage content. But owned by Clever (same parent as Real Estate Witch) — duplicative and the affiliates@ email suggests commercial focus.
candidates.outreach_angle: Your homebuyer guides would be stronger with an embedded affordability calculator — free, no sign-up
candidates.email_caveat: affiliates@ — likely commercial/affiliate department, may not handle editorial embed requests

candidates.name: Wealth of Geeks
candidates.about: Personal finance and lifestyle blog covering money management, investing, and real estate.
candidates.why_fit: General personal finance blog with real estate content. Broad audience. social@ email suggests social media manager — may not be the right channel.
candidates.outreach_angle: General personal finance audience interested in housing costs — embed our free mortgage calculator for your readers
candidates.email_caveat: social@ — social media contact, likely wrong channel for widget embed requests
